source_pr.title: backend: remove unexpected escape in external writer
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #65319

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

During sorting, `i` would be moved to heap, which is caused by `dupLoc`.

https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/1776433ee098d699de8f315c6c9b6434b551ad1e/pkg/lightning/backend/external/writer.go#L568-L578

Below is the result of tests added in this PR. You can see that the allocs/op is extremely high.

```
BenchmarkSortLocationWithEscape-32             2         972900236 ns/op        366561608 B/op  22186617 allocs/op
BenchmarkSortLocationWithoutEscape-32          3         439848517 ns/op        11566922 B/op         18 allocs/op
```

If you run the test with `go test -gcflags="-m"`, you can find this in the output:

```
pkg/lightning/membuf/buffer_test.go:404:31: moved to heap: a
```

Another place we need to sort data is `loadRangeBatchData`. But it use `sorty.Sort`, so there is no such problem.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [X] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [X] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Import 500G data into following table with 500B average row length, and add index `i1(c(64))`

```SQL
CREATE TABLE `test`.`t` (
  `id` bigint NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `k` int NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',
  `c` varbinary(256) NOT NULL DEFAULT '' COMMENT "max_length=120, min_length=120",
  `pad` varbinary(256) NOT NULL DEFAULT '' COMMENT "max_length=120, min_length=120",
  `c1` int NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',
  `c2` int NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',
  `c3` int NOT NULL DEFAULT '0' COMMENT "mean=5000,stddev=30000",
  `c4` date DEFAULT NULL,
  `c5` datetime DEFAULT NULL,
  `c6` double DEFAULT NULL,
  `c7` varbinary(256) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT "max_length=120, min_length=120",
  `c8` varbinary(256) DEFAULT NULL COMMENT "max_length=120, min_length=120",
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`) /*T![clustered_index] CLUSTERED */
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_bin;
```

Below is the result:

|   | read index | ingest|
| ------------- | ------------- | ------------- | 
| Before  | 00:15:29  | 00:11:57 | 
| This PR  | 00:13:42 | 00:12:01 |
| #65137  | 00:10:25 | 00:11:54 |

> The last one is just used to illustrate the cost of getting slice from `SliceLocation`.

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

fix_pattern: During sorting, `i` would be moved to heap, which is caused by `dupLoc`.
analysis:
PR title: backend: remove unexpected escape in external writer
PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/65316
Triage sources: manual_triage, determinism_stabilization
Triage summary: rnd := rand2.New(rand2.NewSource(6716)) (pkg/lightning/membuf/buffer_test.go)

Patch series analyzed; see patch_url.
